title: Spike: ratify CONTAIN (assume-breach / zero-trust tool execution) as the PRIMARY supply-chain control — generalize the deps-scan sandbox into a shared tool-exec lane + CI hardening + ADR
description:
## Problem statement

The lifecycle epic (ADMIT→PIN/VERIFY→DIVERSIFY→MONITOR→RETIRE) is entirely SELECTION + VERIFICATION — every
stage answers "is this tool trustworthy?" But you CANNOT verify a tool/dependency is uncompromised, and 2026
proved verification-by-reputation is defeatable: Mini Shai-Hulud (May 2026; TanStack/Mistral/OpenSearch)
victims had VALID SLSA Build L3 provenance + OIDC trusted-publishing + 2FA and were STILL compromised — the
worm hijacked the LEGITIMATE pipeline. StepSecurity: "provenance confirms WHICH pipeline produced the
artifact, not WHETHER the pipeline was behaving as intended." The only control that stopped it in-flight was
an EGRESS ALLOWLIST. Tool diversity is the cited fix in ZERO 2026 postmortems. So swapping Trivy != security.
The durable control is CONTAINMENT: assume any tool IS backdoored and make the backdoor INERT by denying
network/egress + creds + host access. This is the 2026 consensus (NIST SSDF SP 800-218 / SP 800-204D;
CISA-NSA Defending CI/CD; OpenSSF S2C2F; SLSA L3 hermetic). white-hacker already has the SEED — the Agents
Rule of Two (agents/white-hacker.md:39-41) + the deps-scan-sandbox LOCKDOWN (docker/deps-scan-sandbox/run.sh:
--network none, --read-only+tmpfs, --cap-drop ALL, no-new-privileges, non-root, --pids/--memory, --rm). This
spike ELEVATES containment from a posture line + one-skill sandbox to the PRIMARY control wrapping the whole
lifecycle. Research grounding: docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md (CONTAIN section) +
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (G4 + LBC-4/LBC-5).

## ADR OWNERSHIP (settles the §9.3 collision — recorded 2026-06-09)
**THIS ticket owns THE single strategy ADR** (next-available number AT WRITE TIME — last written ADR-023;
do NOT hard-code): CONTAIN is the PRIMARY supply-chain control; the 5 lifecycle stages are defense-in-depth
under it; the CONTAIN invariant (>=2 of {network/egress, creds-in-env, host-write} ABSENT at every tool
execution) is the registry rule. wh-hxt.2 (runbook + diversity policy) REFERENCES this ADR — it does NOT
append a competing lifecycle ADR. The ADR should also CITE (not re-debate) ADR-007 (opt-in sandboxed PoC
detonation precedent), ADR-016 (defense-in-depth confinement precedent), ADR-003 (degrade-to-floor when
docker absent), ADR-006 (pin) — and CLARIFY, append-only, that ADR-015's "registry self-updates" sentence
is design intent until wh-hxt.4 lands, and that ADR-021's tag-pin lane must resolve tags to commit SHAs
(the force-push lesson) unless wh-562's Gate-2 ADR carries that supersession instead (exactly one of the
two states it; cross-reference from the other).

## Research questions (each -> a decision)
- RQ1 — The CONTAIN invariant: codify ">=2 of {network/egress, creds-in-env, host-write} ABSENT at every
  tool execution" as the registry rule. Which tools in the set (Grype/Syft, Checkov, OSV-Scanner,
  gitleaks, native gates) can run fully offline + no-creds today, and which need a fetch/analyze SPLIT
  (network-on fetch step, network-off analyze step — the pattern deps-scan-sandbox/fetch-snapshot.sh already
  uses)? Produce the per-tool containment matrix.
- RQ2 — Generalize the sandbox: should docker/deps-scan-sandbox/run.sh become a SHARED tool-execution lane
  (a _shared wrapper any capability calls) or stay per-skill with a shared LOCKDOWN[] array? Decide the shape
  that keeps ADR-003 graceful-degrade (no docker -> fall to the Read/Grep/Glob floor, tool_assisted:false).
- RQ3 — THE INNER-LOOP AUTO-ROUTE BRIDGE (promoted from notes — loop-leverage G4): the sandbox is OPT-IN
  today, invoked by NO inner-loop skill or the agent; at a default review S8 degrades to [] with "malware-db"
  in tools_unavailable (supply_chain.py:1122-1124; activation needs hand-passed malware_db, SKILL.md:82).
  Decide: when a pinned snapshot path is configured AND docker is available, auto-route the deps-scan stage
  through run.sh (S8 default-when-safe), else fall back to S1-S7 + tools_unavailable. Decide default-on vs
  louder-opt-in (the user may not want a surprise docker run). PREREQ: build-verify ./run.sh build &&
  ./run.sh test on a daemon host (README.md:87-88 authored-not-build-verified; bd memory records 67 passed
  on Rancher Desktop — re-confirm).
- RQ4 — ARTIFACT provenance-verify at admission (THIS ticket's arm — distinct from wh-562's Gate-2):
  full commit-SHA / image-digest / binary-checksum + cosign/SLSA VERIFY (not generate-and-trust) for the
  TOOLS the agent executes. This is CONTAIN's admission arm and operates on ARTIFACTS. wh-562's Gate-2 is a
  DIFFERENT mechanism for a DIFFERENT object kind: a deterministic DATA gate (per-entry GHSA/OSV provenance
  + OSV-schema + regression) for WATCHLIST/REGISTRY ENTRIES. Cross-reference; do NOT fold them into one
  mechanism (the 2026-06-09 audit corrected the earlier "one mechanism" framing — two object kinds, two gates).
- RQ5 — CI hardening guidance for .github/workflows/ + ci/security-review.action.yml: SHA-pin Actions (not
  tags — the trivy-action force-push), minimal GITHUB_TOKEN (contents:read per-job), OIDC scope-pinned to an
  immutable workflow + protected branch, EGRESS ALLOWLIST (Harden-Runner block mode — the control that
  stopped Mini Shai-Hulud), --ignore-scripts, ephemeral runners, atomic secret rotation. Produce a hardened-
  CI checklist (cite GitHub SHA-pin policy 2025-08-15; EU CRA reporting 2026-09-11 / SBOM-mandatory). Decide
  where it lives: ci/ docs vs ADR content (open decision §9.4).
- RQ6 — The reframe: confirm in the ADR that CONTAIN is PRIMARY and ADMIT/PIN/DIVERSIFY/MONITOR/RETIRE are
  defense-in-depth UNDER it (none survives an undetected compromise; CONTAIN does).

## Constraint (by design — do not "fix")
CONTAIN enforcement code is OUT-of-lane for the outer loop: confine_self_writes FROZEN/CONTROL basenames +
default-deny (:69-77) deny self-rewrite of hooks/gate/sandbox. Improvements arrive as human-PR'd TDD diffs,
never KB text. This asymmetry is identity preservation (Rule 5) — the ADR should state it.

## Investigation plan
1. Read docker/deps-scan-sandbox/{run.sh,fetch-snapshot.sh,README.md} + the deps-scan S8 offline path.
2. Read agents/white-hacker.md:39-41 (Rule of Two) + _shared/reference/tool-registry.md + detect_tools.py.
3. Build the per-tool containment matrix (RQ1) + decide the shared-lane shape (RQ2) + the auto-route bridge
   + its default (RQ3; build-verify run.sh first).
4. Specify the artifact-provenance admission arm (RQ4) — cross-referenced with wh-562's Gate-2, not merged.
5. Produce the hardened-CI checklist + decide its home (RQ5).
6. Draft THE strategy ADR (next-available number AT WRITE TIME) per the ADR-ownership block above.

## Exit criteria (we are done when)
- THE strategy ADR appended to docs/ARD.md (next-free # at write): CONTAIN primary; lifecycle =
  defense-in-depth; the CONTAIN invariant as registry rule; the ADR-015 design-intent + ADR-021 tag-pin
  clarifications carried here or explicitly delegated to wh-562's ADR.
- A per-tool containment matrix + the shared-tool-exec-lane decision (generalize sandbox vs shared LOCKDOWN).
- The auto-route bridge decision (S8 default-when-safe vs louder-opt-in) + run.sh build-verified on a
  daemon host (the prereq for relying on the lane).
- A hardened-CI checklist (SHA-pin / minimal token / OIDC scope / egress-allowlist / --ignore-scripts) +
  a decision on where it lives.
- Impl follow-up ticket(s): (a) the shared lane + live-verify ONE real tool (e.g. Grype) running fully
  offline + no-creds + sandboxed against a pinned snapshot; (b) the auto-route bridge; (c) the CI checklist
  wired into ci/security-review.action.yml. Even a "keep it per-skill" outcome must record the decision.

## Time box
~1-2 days. Research is largely done (the strategy CONTAIN section + the loop-leverage audit); this is
decision + ADR + impl-ticket carving.

## Likely outcomes
- CONTAIN ratified as primary; the deps-scan-sandbox LOCKDOWN lifted to a shared tool-exec lane; S8
  default-when-safe; a backdoored Grype/Checkov becomes inert.
- DIVERSIFY demoted from "the security answer" to blast-radius reduction (defense-in-depth).
- wh-562 stays a SEPARATE, re-scoped Gate-2 DATA-gate spike (cross-referenced, not folded).

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md (CONTAIN section + 2026 sources: StepSecurity Mini
Shai-Hulud postmortem, SLSA L3 hermetic, NIST SP 800-218/204D, CISA-NSA Defending CI/CD, OpenSSF S2C2F,
GitHub SHA-pin policy 2025-08-15, EU CRA). docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (G4,
LBC-4/5, §9 decisions). docker/deps-scan-sandbox/. ADR-001/003/006/007/015/016/019. Siblings: wh-562
(Gate-2 DATA gate — blocked by this), wh-nvk (DIVERSIFY), wh-xn0 (ADMIT), wh-5es (MONITOR), wh-d5b
(RETIRE interim), wh-hxt.2 (runbook — references this ADR), wh-hxt.4 (ADMIT-via-loop).
notes: GROOM 2026-06-10 (pre-claim pass; supersedes the 2026-06-09 loop-wiring note — all cites re-verified at HEAD 338d667): VERDICT READY with 3 corrections. (1) STALE CITE: the S8 degrade is now supply_chain.py:1271-1276 (unavailable.add('malware-db')), NOT :1122-1124 — wave-1a/0871c4f moved it; SKILL.md:81-82 activation cite still good; white-hacker.md:39-41 Rule-of-Two cite still good. (2) RQ3 PREREQ STATE CHANGED TWICE: the sandbox WAS docker-build-verified post-authoring (bd memory: Rancher Desktop/moby, docker 29.1.3, 67 passed 1 deselected) — BUT (a) that 67-count predates wave-1a; the deps-scan package is now 102 tests, so the image must be REBUILT + re-run (expect ~102) before relying on the lane; (b) docker is NOT currently reachable on this host — only the 'default' context exists pointing at /var/run/docker.sock (not exposed here; the rancher-desktop context is unregistered, i.e. Rancher Desktop not running). OPERATOR ACTION before RQ3's build-verify: start Rancher Desktop (re-registers the context), then ./run.sh build && ./run.sh test. RQ3's DESIGN work (auto-route decision) does NOT block on this — only the live verification does. (3) ALL else verified: sandbox files exist; ci/security-review.action.yml + .github/workflows/ci.yml exist (RQ5 targets); last written ADR = ADR-023 (claim holds, nothing reserved); all 7 cited ADRs resolve; no blockers; blocks wh-562 + wh-hxt.2 as designed. Spike = research+ADR+ticket-carving, no package code (no test baseline of its own; deps-scan baseline 102 green per wh-7o1 groom).

title: EPIC: supply-chain-resilient tooling — a self-correcting capability lifecycle (admit→pin/verify→diversify→monitor→retire)
description:
## Goal
Govern white-hacker's capability TOOLS so that a COMPROMISE of any tool — Trivy, its replacement, or one we
have not picked yet — cannot harm us, EVEN in the window before the advisory drops. The load-bearing control
is CONTAINMENT (assume-breach / zero-trust execution): run every tool offline + no-creds + sandboxed +
provenance-verified, so a backdoor is INERT. ON TOP of that, a SELF-CORRECTING LIFECYCLE makes tool
compromise *and* staleness normal, gated, reversible events. Strategy:
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md · Loop-leverage audit + handoff:
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (the lifecycle RIDES the existing inner/outer
self-improvement loops — wire them, don't build bespoke machinery).

## Why (swapping is NOT the solution)
Trivy compromised (TeamPCP, CVE-2026-33634) + trivy-mcp unmaintained/stale-pinned. A point-swap is a
TREADMILL AND not security: you cannot know whether the alternatives are also compromised. Mini Shai-Hulud
(May 2026) victims had VALID SLSA Build L3 provenance + OIDC + 2FA and were STILL compromised; the only
control that stopped it in-flight was an EGRESS ALLOWLIST. Tool diversity is the cited fix in ZERO 2026
postmortems. CONTAINMENT (not a better tool list) is the durable answer.

## PRIMARY control: CONTAIN (assume-breach / zero-trust execution) → wh-hxt.3
The CONTAIN invariant: at least 2 of {network/egress, creds-in-env, host-write} ABSENT at every tool
execution. Seeds exist: the Agents Rule of Two (agents/white-hacker.md:39-41) + the deps-scan-sandbox
LOCKDOWN (docker/deps-scan-sandbox/run.sh). wh-hxt.3 ratifies THE strategy ADR, generalizes the sandbox to
a shared tool-exec lane, decides the S8 auto-route bridge (the sandbox is opt-in today — not invoked by any
inner-loop skill), and produces the hardened-CI checklist.

## SETTLED COORDINATION DECISIONS (2026-06-09 — children must honor these)
1. **ADR ownership (the §9.3 collision):** wh-hxt.3 owns THE single strategy ADR (CONTAIN-primary +
   lifecycle-as-defense-in-depth + the invariant). wh-hxt.2 references it (runbook + diversity policy — no
   competing ADR). wh-562 owns ONE watchlist-mechanism ADR (Gate-1 vs Gate-2 + schema + write-lane) — wh-5es's
   ADR deliverable FOLDS IN there. wh-xn0 (admissibility) + wh-nvk (drop-Trivy) keep their own. Six planned
   ADRs collapsed to FOUR; every number computed at write time (last written ADR-023; NOTHING reserved).
2. **TWO GATES, two object kinds:** Gate-1 = the eval keep/revert (KB/checklist text edits — what the corpus
   can score). Gate-2 = deterministic per-entry GHSA/OSV provenance + OSV-schema + regression (watchlist/
   registry DATA edits — the eval gate STRUCTURALLY cannot score these; reusing it = a false-merit merge).
   ARTIFACT provenance (tool binaries: SHA/digest/checksum+cosign) is a THIRD thing — wh-hxt.3's RQ4, not Gate-2.
3. **ONE watchlist schema:** the OSV-superset — wh-5es RQ2 FINALIZES it, wh-k6l INSTANTIATES the file,
   wh-562's Gate-2 VALIDATES it. No bespoke tool-keyed shape; tools are entries with target:tool.
4. **The registry rewrite is ONE coordinated change:** wh-xn0 (columns) + wh-nvk (rows) + wh-hxt.1 (health
   column) + wh-hxt.2 (policy text) all touch tool-registry.md + SCANNER_PREFERENCE — combined or explicitly
   sequenced after wh-nvk; never uncoordinated parallel writers.
5. **CONTAIN code is out-of-lane for self-edit** (confine FROZEN/CONTROL + default-deny) — improvements via
   human-PR'd TDD diffs, never KB text. By design (Rule 5); the strategy ADR states it.

## DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH: the 5-stage lifecycle (UNDER CONTAIN), riding the existing loops
1. **ADMIT** — license/egress/maintenance/integrity gates → wh-xn0 (+ staleness gate wh-hxt.1) + the
   ADMIT-via-loop proposer → **wh-hxt.4** (the registry-row writer; sec-kb-refresh primary arm — the
   "registry self-updates" promise of ADR-015, aspirational until this lands)
2. **PIN+VERIFY** — artifact provenance (SHA/digest/cosign) → wh-hxt.3 RQ4; watchlist-DATA integrity →
   wh-562 Gate-2
3. **DIVERSIFY** — blast-radius reduction, NOT prevention → wh-nvk (the replacement set) + the policy (wh-hxt.2)
4. **MONITOR** — the OSSF feeder into sec-kb-refresh (poll_feeds has NO OSSF parser today) + the S8
   version-aware fix → wh-5es (feeder + schema) + wh-4k9 (S8) + wh-k6l (the file) + staleness (wh-hxt.1)
5. **RETIRE+REPLACE** — quarantine→select→record→retire, reusable → wh-d5b (stopgap, iac-only edit) →
   wh-nvk (permanent) + the runbook (wh-hxt.2) + tool/watchlist retire paths (wh-hxt.1)

## Execution Waves (MANDATORY table — one wave = one `/launch-team <ids…>`; deps wired in bd)

| Wave | Ticket | One-line | Type | Blocked by |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | wh-4k9 * | S8 version-aware match (resolved-version extraction first) — do FIRST, smallest win | code | — |
| 1 | wh-hxt.3 | THE strategy ADR + sandbox→shared lane + S8 auto-route bridge + CI checklist | spike | — |
| 1 | wh-d5b | Trivy stopgap: the ONE iac reorder (detect_tools.py:116) + registry caveat | code | — |
| 1 | wh-xn0 | admissibility gates (license/egress) + registry-column spec | spike | — |
| 1 | wh-5es | OSSF feeder design + FINALIZE the OSV-superset schema | spike | — |
| 1 | wh-q86 * | KB AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 in a NEW supply-chain-2.md | doc | — |
| 2 | wh-562 | Gate-2 DATA gate + write-lane + the ONE watchlist-mechanism ADR | spike | wh-hxt.3 |
| 2 | wh-k6l * | the curated OSV watchlist file + loader-merge + append process | code | wh-4k9 (path per wh-562 RQ-B) |
| 2 | wh-nvk | replacement-set scorecard + permanent Trivy removal spec | spike | wh-d5b |
| 3 | wh-hxt.4 | ADMIT-via-loop: registry sidecar + arm-agnostic row writer | spike | wh-562 |
| 3 | wh-hxt.2 | retire→replace runbook + diversity-by-design policy (no ADR) | doc | wh-hxt.3, wh-nvk |
| 3 | wh-hxt.1 | staleness gate/signal + tool/watchlist RETIRE paths | code | (sequence after the wh-nvk/wh-xn0 registry rewrite) |

\* = hot-path sibling, not an epic child (wh-4k9, wh-k6l, wh-q86) — included because the waves are
launched as a program; they appear in exactly one wave each.

**Wave-validity caveats (coordination handoffs, per the epic template):** (a) Wave 1 has THREE
ADR-appending spikes (wh-hxt.3, wh-xn0; wh-5es feeds wh-562's) — `docs/ARD.md` is append-only and
numbers are taken at write time, so appends serialize trivially; do not parallel-edit the file itself.
(b) wh-d5b and wh-xn0 both reference tool-registry.md, but wh-d5b edits it while wh-xn0 only SPECS
columns (its impl lands with wh-nvk's rewrite) — disjoint in practice. (c) Launch each wave via
`/launch-team <ids…>`; end with `/handoff wave-N-wh-hxt`.

## Definition of Done
- THE strategy ADR ratified (CONTAIN primary; lifecycle defense-in-depth; the invariant as registry rule).
- The sandbox lane generalized + ONE real tool live-verified offline+no-creds+sandboxed; the S8 auto-route
  decision made (build-verified run.sh).
- Gate-2 exists (validator + write-lane); the ONE OSV-superset watchlist is seeded, fed (OSSF feeder), and
  version-aware (no name-only FP bomb).
- A hardened-CI checklist exists; ADMIT includes maintenance; RETIRE covers tools + watchlist entries;
  diversity-by-design stated with the honest blast-radius framing; the RETIRE→REPLACE runbook documented
  (Trivy worked through it).
- The registry self-update arm (wh-hxt.4) designed with its impl ticket carved.

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md · 20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (the
research+handoff record: leverage map, G1-G6, verified claims incl. the LBC-6 correction) ·
20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md · 20260609_supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md ·
ADR-001/002/003/006/007/015/016/019/022/023.

title: Curated EXTENSIBLE known-compromised watchlist (git-tracked OSV, loader-merged, append-on-new-advisory) — seed with current entries
description:
## Goal
Build the EXTENSIBLE MECHANISM for a curated, git-tracked known-compromised watchlist that deps-scan's
S8 checks — NOT a one-off TeamPCP snapshot. The deliverable is the **format + the loader-merge + a
documented, human-gated APPEND process**, so EVERY future compromise (not just today's) is a small dated
entry someone can add in minutes. Seed it with the current confirmed entries as the FIRST batch.

## Why (re-scoped per review)
Hard-coding "the TeamPCP campaign" is too specific — compromises are continuous. The watchlist must be a
LIVING, append-only list with a clear "a new advisory dropped → add an entry" path. This ticket is the
manual-curated FOUNDATION; wh-5es generalizes it (extensions, digests, multi-surface) and AUTOMATES the
updates (the sec-kb-refresh OSSF feeder — note: that feeder is design-intent today, poll_feeds has no
OSSF parser; this ticket's manual append process is the ONLY working path until wh-5es lands). The
entries below are DATA/examples, not the point.

## COORDINATION (added 2026-06-09 — loop-leverage G2; agree BEFORE creating the file so it never moves)
- **Schema:** the file instantiates the OSV-superset that **wh-5es RQ2 finalizes** — plain OSV that
  load_malware_db (malware_db.py:26) already parses for the package kind; tools enter as entries with a
  target:tool tag (NOT a bespoke tool-keyed shape — that was dropped).
- **Gate:** every entry (seed batch included) must pass **wh-562's Gate-2 validator** — per-entry
  GHSA/OSV source URL + OSV-schema validity + regression-green.
- **Write-lane:** the planned home `plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/reference/` is NOT in
  confine_self_writes ALLOW_SEGMENTS (:40) — the outer loop cannot write there today. The path vs an
  ALLOW_SEGMENTS extension is **wh-562 RQ-B's decision** — confirm it before step 1.

## Prerequisites
- DEPENDS ON wh-4k9 (S8 version-aware match) — else popular packages FP-flag every user.
- load_malware_db (malware_db.py:26) parses OSV docs → {name:set[versions]}; OSV is the interop standard
  (GHSA / OSV.dev / OSSF all export it), so the curated file scales + the future automated feed appends
  in the SAME shape. Shares the Trivy entry with wh-562.

## Steps
1. Confirm the file path with wh-562 RQ-B (write-lane decision), THEN add the GENERICALLY-NAMED curated
   watchlist `known-compromised.osv.json` (or a small dir, one file per ecosystem) — OSV format; each
   entry dated + carrying its advisory source/url. NOT named after any one campaign.
2. Loader merge: load_malware_db (or the supply_chain.py caller) reads the curated file IN ADDITION to
   the optional big OSSF snapshot (union the maps); never-raise/degrade preserved.
3. Document the APPEND PROCESS + an entry TEMPLATE in reference/MALWARE-DB.md: "when a supply-chain
   compromise is confirmed (GHSA/OSV/OSSF), add a dated OSV entry via a human-gated draft-PR (ADR-012),
   Gate-2-validated: name, ecosystem, exact bad versions, source URL, retrieved date."
4. SEED the FIRST dated batch (2026-06, each sourced): litellm [1.82.7,1.82.8] (GHSA-5mg7-485q-xm76);
   telnyx [4.87.1,4.87.2]; @tanstack/* (the compromised set — cite the postmortem); trivy [0.69.4]
   (GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23). Data, not logic.
5. Tests: the loader merges the curated file; a seeded bad version flags (no OSSF snapshot needed); a
   safe version does NOT (needs wh-4k9); **EXTENSIBILITY: a NEW fixture entry added to the file flags
   WITHOUT any code change.**

## Files to Modify/Create
- NEW plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/reference/known-compromised.osv.json (path per wh-562 RQ-B)
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/malware_db.py (merge) or the caller
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/reference/MALWARE-DB.md (the append process + template)
- tests

## Verification
- package tests green
- litellm==1.82.7 in a lockfile, NO OSSF snapshot → S8 HIGH supply-chain finding
- a SAFE version → no flag (relies on wh-4k9)
- extensibility test: a new (name, version) fixture entry flags with NO code edit
- the curated file validates as OSV (and passes Gate-2 once wh-562's validator exists); ships in the
  vendor manifest (deps-scan is a consumer skill)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] a GENERICALLY-NAMED git-tracked OSV watchlist + loader merge (NOT campaign-specific)
- [ ] file path confirmed with wh-562 RQ-B before creation (write-lane decision honored)
- [ ] MALWARE-DB.md documents the human-gated, Gate-2-validated APPEND process + an entry template
- [ ] seeded with the current confirmed entries, each dated + sourced (data, not logic)
- [ ] adding a new entry needs NO code change (extensibility test passes)
- [ ] reconciles with wh-5es (one watchlist; this instantiates its RQ2 schema) + shares the Trivy entry with wh-562
- [ ] safe versions don't flag (relies on wh-4k9); tests green

## Rollback
`git revert` — remove the curated file + the merge.

## References
docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md + _supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md +
_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (G2 + §9.1); malware_db.py:26,47; confine_self_writes.py:40; OSV format
(osv.dev); ADR-012, ADR-019; wh-5es (schema), wh-562 (Gate-2 + write-lane), wh-4k9 (prereq).

title: deps-scan S8: version-aware match (fix the name-only false-positive bomb)
description:
## Goal
Fix the S8 false-positive bomb. `signal_s8` (**supply_chain.py:754** — corrected from :744) matches a
dependency by NAME ONLY (`if d["name"] in malware_db`), ignoring the version — so a watchlist entry for a
*popular* compromised package (LiteLLM, @tanstack/*) would flag EVERY user of that package, not just the
bad versions. Make S8 version-aware so it only flags the actually-installed bad version.
(docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md RQ3; implements wh-5es RQ3; loop-leverage
audit G3 — twice-confirmed: is_known_bad is DEAD CODE with zero production callers.)

## Prerequisites
- `is_known_bad(name, version, db)` exists (malware_db.py:47-52), matches name+version (or `"*"`), and

title: Spike: Gate-2 — deterministic DATA gate for watchlist/registry edits (per-entry GHSA/OSV provenance + OSV-schema + write-lane); Trivy/TeamPCP RQ1-RQ2 FINAL
description:
> **RE-SCOPED 2026-06-09** per the loop-leverage audit (docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md
> §4.1 + §5.1/LBC-6). The original RQ1 (verify) + RQ2 (exposure) are **FINAL** in
> docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md — do not re-research. The original "binary
> checksum-verify is OUR load-bearing control" framing was CORRECTED: the OSSF snapshot pin is ALREADY
> SHA-verified in code (docker/deps-scan-sandbox/fetch-snapshot.sh:16 40-hex enforce, :34 clone, :36
> checkout, :37 `git rev-parse HEAD == PIN || exit 1`); ARTIFACT provenance (tool binaries/images:
> commit-SHA/digest/checksum + cosign verify) is wh-hxt.3's RQ4 (CONTAIN's admission arm). The
> Trivy-return/reversibility framing is DEAD (wh-nvk's drop decision). What remains — and what this spike
> now IS — is **Gate-2: the deterministic DATA gate for supply-chain watchlist/registry edits.**

## Problem statement

The outer loop's eval keep/revert gate (Gate-1) STRUCTURALLY cannot score a watchlist or tool-registry
DATA edit: evals/score.py:64-95 consumes only findings-vs-label.json, and no corpus case measures "did
adding compromised-package X help" — so a poisoned or wrong-version entry could never be REVERTed on
merit, and an eval-J KEEP earned by an unrelated KB change must never smuggle a DATA row through
(false-merit merge). Today: (a) load_malware_db (malware_db.py:26-44) is a pure offline loader that
never verifies an entry against a primary source; (b) NO watchlist-entry validator exists (validate_kb.py
gates KB entries only); (c) the watchlist's planned home (deps-scan/reference/) is NOT in
confine_self_writes ALLOW_SEGMENTS (:40), so the outer loop cannot even write it; (d) gate_kb_edit
demands a gate-verdict.json==KEEP for /_shared/reference/ writes — the wrong verdict kind for DATA.

## Research questions (each -> a decision)
- RQ-A — THE GATE-2 VALIDATOR: a deterministic script (no LLM/RNG — Rule 5) mirroring validate_kb.py
  that gates every watchlist/registry DATA entry on: (1) REQUIRED primary-source advisory URL (GHSA/OSV)
  per entry; (2) OSV-JSON schema validity against a pinned schema; (3) regression-green (the existing
  malware_db/deps-scan tests + the version-aware predicate exercised). Decide its home: deps-scan/scripts/
  (next to malware_db.py) vs _shared/scripts/ as a reusable DATA-gate (open decision §9.1 — leaning
  _shared if wh-hxt.4's registry rows use the same gate).
- RQ-B — THE WRITE-LANE: extend confine_self_writes ALLOW_SEGMENTS (or relocate the watchlist file) so
  the outer loop CAN write watchlist entries — under Gate-2, NOT ungated. Decide the verdict mechanism:
  Gate-2's checks mint the verdict file for DATA edits, or a parallel gate hook handles the watchlist
  path; NEVER reuse an eval-J KEEP for a DATA row. Coordinate the file path with wh-k6l BEFORE the file
  is created (so it doesn't move).
- RQ-C — ONE WATCHLIST SCHEMA (ownership settled 2026-06-09): the schema IS the OSV-superset — wh-5es
  RQ2 FINALIZES it (ecosystem + extension/target kinds), wh-k6l INSTANTIATES the concrete file, THIS
  spike's Gate-2 VALIDATES it. The original bespoke tool-keyed shape ({tool:{bad_versions,bad_digests}})
  is DROPPED — tools enter as OSV entries with target:tool, not a second format. One file/feed/updater.
- RQ-D — THE ADR: ONE watchlist-mechanism ADR (next-available number AT WRITE TIME — last written
  ADR-023; NOTHING is reserved) covering Gate-1 vs Gate-2 (which gate governs which edit kind), the
  shared OSV-superset schema, and the write-lane. wh-5es's planned monitoring ADR FOLDS IN here (one
  watchlist -> one ADR; its design feeds this). The ADR's Context should cite ADR-001/004/022 as the
  source of the single-gate framing it refines (their "gated by the eval keep-or-revert" wording is
  scoped to KB edits only — append-only clarification, never edit them). If wh-hxt.3's strategy ADR did
  not carry the ADR-021 tag-pin supersession, this ADR states it (exactly one of the two does).
- RQ-E — KB entry: AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 (the TeamPCP technique) is DELEGATED to wh-q86 (exists).

## What already exists (do NOT rebuild)
- Snapshot-pin verification: fetch-snapshot.sh (SHA-enforced clone+checkout+verify) — done.
- Interim quarantine: wh-d5b. Permanent removal + replacement set: wh-nvk. The curated watchlist file +
  loader-merge + append process: wh-k6l. The OSSF feeder into sec-kb-refresh: wh-5es. The KB entry: wh-q86.
  This spike delivers the GATE + the write-lane + the one ADR.

## Exit criteria
- Gate-2 validator decided (home + checks + verdict mechanism) with an impl follow-up ticket.
- The write-lane decision recorded (ALLOW_SEGMENTS extension vs file relocation), coordinated with wh-k6l.
- The ONE watchlist-mechanism ADR appended (next-free number at write time), folding in wh-5es's ADR
  deliverable and stating the Gate-1/Gate-2 split.
- No bespoke tool-keyed schema anywhere (OSV-superset only).

## Time box
~1 day (research is done; this is design + ADR + impl-ticket carving). BLOCKED BY wh-hxt.3 (the strategy
ADR lands first; this ADR builds on it).

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (§4.1 Gate-2, §5.1 LBC-6 correction, G2);
docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md (RQ1/RQ2 FINAL);
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_tooling_strategy.md. evals/score.py:64-95; malware_db.py:26-44,47-52;
confine_self_writes.py:40; gate_kb_edit.py:18,40-49; validate_kb.py (the pattern). Siblings: wh-hxt.3
(blocks this; artifact provenance + strategy ADR), wh-5es (schema finalize + feeder; ADR folds in here),
wh-k6l (the file), wh-4k9 (version-aware S8), wh-q86 (KB entry), wh-xn0/wh-nvk (their own ADRs).
notes: LOOP-WIRING AUDIT 2026-06-09 (workflow wst324yuh, adversarially verified): the eval keep/revert gate (Gate-1) STRUCTURALLY cannot score a watchlist/registry DATA edit — score.py:64-95 consumes only findings-vs-label.json; no corpus label for "did adding compromised-package X help". So watchlist edits need a SECOND deterministic gate (Gate-2): per-ENTRY GHSA/OSV source URL REQUIRED + OSV-schema validity + regression-green, mirroring validate_kb.py (no LLM/RNG, Rule 5). CORRECTION (an earlier overclaim was REFUTED): the SNAPSHOT pin IS already SHA-verified in code — fetch-snapshot.sh:16,34,36,37 (40-hex SHA enforce -> git clone -> checkout PIN -> `git rev-parse HEAD == PIN || exit 1`); MALWARE-DB.md:48 SHA is the operator RECORD of that pin. So the integrity gap is NOT the snapshot checksum (exists) but (a) per-entry provenance verify at load time — malware_db.py:26-44 load_malware_db never verifies a primary source; (b) deps-scan watchlist path NOT in confine_self_writes ALLOW_SEGMENTS (:40) so the outer loop cannot write it. Pairs with wh-k6l + wh-5es.

title: Supply-chain-malware floor — generic capability, npm adapter first
description: Add an OFFLINE, static heuristic 'supply-chain-malware floor' behind the existing SCA capability (ADR-015) to catch NOVEL malicious/typosquatted packages that CVE-based SCA (npm audit/OSV-by-CVE) structurally misses. Ecosystem-AGNOSTIC by design: a shared signal core + a per-ecosystem adapter that normalizes that ecosystem's manifest+lockfile+hooks. Ship the generic core + the npm adapter FIRST (npm incidents are critical now); PyPI/RubyGems/Go/Cargo/Maven are follow-on adapters behind the same interface. Emits low/medium-confidence tool_assisted:false candidates; NEVER blocks; triage+human decide. Grounded in spike-09 (S1-S8 + scoring + remediation ladder) and its F5 generalization.
design: Architecture: (1) pure-function signal core (Rule 5, stdlib-only) over a normalized struct {deps:[{name,spec,source_type}], lifecycle_scripts, lockfile_present, script_files}. (2) npm adapter parses package.json + package-lock/pnpm-lock/yarn.lock into that struct. (3) Signals S1 lifecycle-hook present, S2 non-registry source dep, S3 unpinned+no-lockfile, S4 typosquat vs reference/ allowlist, S5 homoglyph/separator confusion, S6 dangerous-API strings in install script, S7 obfuscation markers, S8 known-bad vs OPTIONAL offline OSSF malicious-packages snapshot (hook; degrades when absent). (4) Scoring: emit on any HIGH (S5/S6/S8) OR >=2 corroborating; lone S1/S3 informational only. (5) Output reuses normalize_deps.py finding shape + degradation.py (category supply-chain, owasp A06:2021, access_required unknown, verified static_review_only, tool_assisted false, capped confidence); recommendation maps to a spike-09 F4 remediation rung. (6) Allowlist + ecosystem map live in deps-scan/reference/ so /sec-kb-refresh extends them (ADR-015).

title: deps-scan: extend version-aware S8 beyond npm/Python — Go v-prefix fix (FN, probed), Gemfile.lock + Cargo.lock resolved extraction
description:
## Goal / Problem
wh-4k9 made S8 version-aware, but resolved-version extraction covers only npm (package-lock v1/v2/v3)
and Python (poetry.lock/uv.lock + the name-stripped == pin via _constraint). Extend it so the repo's
other first-class ecosystems get the same quality. PROBED 2026-06-09/10 (post-wave-1a):
- **Go — a real FN, fix FIRST:** parse_go emits spec "v1.2.3" (_GO_REQUIRE_RE), but _exact_pin requires
  `^\d` → None → a known-bad Go module at its EXACT listed bad version does NOT specific-match (probe:
  db {"github.com/evil/mod": {"v1.2.3"}} + spec "v1.2.3" → []); only wildcard fires. go.mod require
  versions are effectively the selected versions (post `go mod tidy`); OSV Go entries are v-prefixed.
- **Java/Maven:** parse_maven emits the correct OSV `groupId:artifactId` name; literal `<version>`
  specific-matches (log4j probe ✓). But `<version>${log4j.version}</version>` (centralized props) →
  wildcard-only. Resolve `${prop}` against the SAME pom's `<properties>` statically. BOM/parent-managed
  (no `<version>`) stays wildcard-only BY DESIGN (parent pom may be outside the repo).
- **Ruby:** exact Gemfile pins match (probe ✓); idiomatic `~> 1.2.3` → wildcard-only; Gemfile.lock NOT
  parsed (presence-only) — plain text `specs:` section, stdlib-regex parseable.
- **Cargo:** Cargo.lock not parsed (TOML — the existing defensive tomllib path). CAUTION: a bare
  Cargo.toml spec "1.2.3" semantically means ^1.2.3 (a RANGE) — once Cargo.lock parsing lands, prefer
  resolved; the full per-ecosystem exact-pin semantics needs the wh-5es RQ2 ecosystem key (coordinate,
  don't block).
- Minor: Pipfile.lock (JSON) — cheap add.
OUT OF SCOPE: the Gradle floor adapter (a whole missing manifest format) = wh-0c2.

## Background / Context
wh-4k9 (commit 0fa17a8) + the verifier fix (0871c4f) are the pattern: additive optional `resolved` key,
never-raise, stdlib-only, conservative degrade. The watchlist these matches run against is wh-k6l.

## Workflow (Red / Green / Refactor)
Per ecosystem: RED — the probe from this ticket as a failing test (==/!= pair) → GREEN — minimal
extraction/match change → REFACTOR — shared shape with _resolved_npm/_resolved_pypi.

## Steps
1. Go: accept a leading `v` in _exact_pin (return AS WRITTEN so OSV-Go v-prefixed sets match) AND/OR
   attach go.mod require versions as `resolved`.
2. Maven: resolve `${prop}` from the same pom's `<properties>` during the existing ET parse.
3. Ruby: parse Gemfile.lock `specs:` ("    name (1.2.3)") → `resolved` on gem deps.
4. Cargo: parse Cargo.lock `[[package]]` → `resolved`; docstring-flag the bare-spec-is-a-range nuance.
5. Pipfile.lock if trivial.

## Files to Create/Modify
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/supply_chain.py (parse_go/_exact_pin · parse_maven ·
  parse_gem + a _resolved_gem · parse_cargo + a _resolved_cargo)
- plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests/ (extend test_s8_version_aware.py per ecosystem)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Go: spec "v1.2.3" + db {"v1.2.3"} → flags; + db {"v9.9.9"} → does not (the probe inverts)
- [ ] Maven: `${prop}`-versioned dep + same-pom property + matching db → flags; unresolvable prop →
      wildcard-only (== and != pinned)
- [ ] Ruby: `~> 1.2.3` + Gemfile.lock resolving 1.2.9 + db {1.2.9} → flags; db {1.2.3} → does not
- [ ] Cargo: Cargo.lock version preferred over the bare manifest spec; nuance documented
- [ ] All extraction additive (`resolved` key only); never-raise on malformed lockfiles; stdlib-only
- [ ] Package gate green (baseline 102): nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q

## Quality Gates (must pass before `bd close`)
(white-hacker per-package gates — the repo's real gates, not the template's generic ruff/mypy example)
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts --with jsonschema --with pytest pytest plugins/white-hacker/skills/deps-scan/scripts/tests -q` → green (baseline 102; serial, never `-n auto`)
- [ ] `nice -n 10 uv run python packaging/validate_manifest.py .` → OK

## QA Before Push
- [ ] Happy path: the ticket's probe cases pass end-to-end through `scan()` (not just unit helpers)
- [ ] Edge cases: empty/malformed/hostile manifest+lockfile inputs degrade, never raise
- [ ] Error handling: no new exception path; degrade contracts (`tools_unavailable`, empty struct) intact
- [ ] No regressions: the full deps-scan suite + the ==/!= pairs added by wh-4k9/0871c4f stay green

## Verification
The AC probes ARE the verification; each lands as a committed test through the REAL adapter (the
wave-1a lesson: direct helper tests missed the adapter-shape FN).

## Rollback
git revert — additive extraction + matching changes only; no schema/contract change.

## Commit Message Format
Repo rule (cite, don't restate): `feat(deps-scan): …` / `fix(deps-scan): …`; author `Jaigouk Kim <ping@jaigouk.kim>`; NO AI attribution (CLAUDE.md Policy 12).

## Risks / Dependencies
- FILE OVERLAP: wh-8ci, wh-ln2, wh-0c2 also modify `supply_chain.py` — NOT parallel-safe; sequence
  these four (never the same `/launch-team` wave; disjoint File Ownership rule).
- Cargo exact-pin semantics need the per-dep ecosystem key — owned by wh-5es RQ2 (coordinate, don't block).
- Go "return AS WRITTEN" depends on OSV-Go v-prefixed version strings (verified) — re-check if the
  watchlist (wh-k6l) normalizes versions differently.

## Notes
Storage/Schema: N/A (no on-disk artifact change). Sequencing: independent of wh-k6l/wh-0c2; pairs
naturally with wh-k6l seeding.

## References
wh-4k9 (0fa17a8), the verifier fix (0871c4f — _constraint precedent); the Go fix site is
_exact_pin (supply_chain.py:747; regex _EXACT_VERSION_RE :742); dispatch table _DISPATCH (:704);
supply_chain.py (_GO_REQUIRE_RE, parse_maven, _GEM_RE, parse_cargo), wh-5es RQ2 (ecosystem key), wh-0c2 (Gradle sibling), wh-k6l.
notes: GROOM 2026-06-10 (pre-claim pass at HEAD 338d667, post-0871c4f): VERDICT READY. Re-probed every load-bearing claim live: (1) Go FN STANDS — _exact_pin('v1.2.3')→None; exact-bad-Go-version → [] (only wildcard fires); (2) Maven ${prop}→wildcard-only, literal flags (groupId:artifactId naming correct); (3) pypi name-embedded 'evil-pkg==1.2.3' NOW FLAGS (0871c4f fixed the wave-1a verifier major — consistent with the ticket citing it as the _constraint precedent, nothing to re-do); (4) Gemfile.lock/Cargo.lock still presence-only — CITE CORRECTION: Gemfile.lock presence check is supply_chain.py:533 (NOT :452; drifted across wave-1a edits), Cargo.lock :643. Symbol cites EXACT at HEAD: _GO_REQUIRE_RE :544 · _DISPATCH :704 · _EXACT_VERSION_RE :742 · _exact_pin :747 · _constraint :764. Baseline GREEN: 102 passed (serial, nice -n 10) — matches the AC baseline. No blockers (bd ready ✓). REMINDER (Risks section): Lane D is SERIAL — wh-8ci/wh-ln2/wh-0c2 also modify supply_chain.py; this ticket runs alone in its launch; wh-k6l must keep its loader-merge in malware_db.py to stay disjoint.

title: Spike: ADMIT-via-loop — machine-readable tool-registry sidecar + a deterministic registry-row proposal writer (sec-kb-refresh primary arm), behind confine + a DATA-gate verdict
description:
## Problem statement

"The registry self-updates" (ADR-015; the two-loop diagram; tool-registry.md:6-7) is ASPIRATIONAL in
code — the loop-leverage audit (G5) + a first-hand sec-learn audit (2026-06-09, workflow wdf3tkjjp)
confirmed: poll_feeds.py renders KB technique entries ONLY (VALID_CLASSES :23 has no tool/supply-chain
class; no registry writer); sec-learn's patch_merge.merge_markdown_patch (patch_merge.py:40-58) is a
pure, tested str->str section-keyed merge with NO file I/O, NO CLI, and NO non-test caller repo-wide;
tool-registry.md is PROSE (entry-field conventions + a change-log format) with no machine-readable
sidecar and no validator analogous to validate_kb.py. The write-LANE is real (confine_self_writes
ALLOW_SEGMENTS includes /_shared/reference/ — the :38-39 comment says exactly "so sec-learn can PROPOSE
checklist/tool-registry diffs"), and gate_kb_edit gates that segment fail-closed. This spike designs the
missing PROPOSER + the machine-readable substrate, so a new/retired tool self-admits as a gated, dated
diff the way KB techniques do.

## Verified facts to design around (do NOT re-derive)
- **sec-kb-refresh is the PRIMARY proposing arm** — tool discovery is feed-shaped (a new scanner
  announced/compromised), matching its deterministic poll->extract->render->validate pipeline; its
  SKILL.md:34-37 already claims tool proposals (prose-only today).
- **sec-learn is at best a SECONDARY, experience-driven arm and is structurally DRY:** the T-8.3 capture
  hooks exist as tested scripts (capture_hooks.py + wrappers) but are registered in NO hooks.json /
  settings.json (pending human-auth — docs/plan/phase-8-self-improvement.md:107), and evals/traces/ does
  not exist on disk — a /sec-learn run today harvests 0 rows; its ">=3 sessions" pre-gate (SKILL.md:27)
  is prose AND unsatisfiable. Do NOT block this spike on sec-learn wiring — design the writer ARM-AGNOSTIC.
- **The gate mismatch must be resolved explicitly:** gate_kb_edit.py:18 demands gate-verdict.json==KEEP
  for ANY /_shared/reference/ write, but a registry row has no corpus-measurable merit (Gate-2 finding,
  LBC-2) — an eval-J KEEP earned by an unrelated KB change must never smuggle a registry row through.
  Either wh-562's Gate-2 deterministic checks mint the verdict for DATA edits, or a parallel gate hook
  handles the registry path. BLOCKED BY wh-562 (the Gate-2 design is this spike's input).
- **patch_merge gets its first real caller:** the row renderer's merge step should be
  merge_markdown_patch (section-keyed change-log append fits the registry's 'YYYY-MM-DD · +/- ·
  capability · tool · source · rationale' format) — the function was built for exactly this.
- **The admissibility screen comes from wh-xn0 (RQ5 spec):** license in {MIT, Apache-2.0} AND
  data_egress local/telemetry-off — a deterministic pure check (Policy 5) the writer runs BEFORE
  proposing; a failing tool is rejected with a stated reason.

## Research questions (each -> a decision)
- RQ1 — The machine-readable substrate: a sidecar (JSON/YAML) vs structured front-matter in
  tool-registry.md, carrying per-tool fields {capability, tool, license+SPDX-URL, data_egress,
  maintenance/health, pin (commit-SHA/digest/checksum), compromise-status, source-URL, dated}. Keep
  test_registry_lock.py (doc<->SCANNER_PREFERENCE capability drift-lock) green; prefer fields the lock
  test + wh-hxt.1's health column + wh-xn0's columns can share (ONE schema change, not three).
- RQ2 — The row writer: a deterministic script in sec-kb-refresh/scripts/ (primary arm) that renders a
  registry-row candidate {tool, capability, source-url, license, pin, status} -> the sidecar entry + a
  merge_markdown_patch change-log line -> a draft PR. Add a 'tool'/'supply-chain' class to the
  poll_feeds taxonomy or a parallel emit path (decide; :84 hard-asserts VALID_CLASSES today).
- RQ3 — The verdict path: how a registry-row PR gets its gate verdict (wh-562 Gate-2 checks: schema-valid
  + admissibility-screen pass + lock-test green => mint the DATA verdict; never an eval-J KEEP).
- RQ4 — RETIRE rows: the same writer proposes status-changes (compromised/stale/retired — input from
  wh-hxt.1's staleness signal + the watchlist), not just additions.
- RQ5 — sec-learn's secondary arm (DEFERRED unless cheap): IF the capture hooks get registered (a human
  decision — flag it, do not do it here), encode the >=3-sessions threshold in code over harvest.py's
  by_session output (:31-37). Out of scope otherwise.

## Exit criteria
- The sidecar/front-matter schema decided + the ONE registry-schema change coordinated with
  wh-xn0 (columns) + wh-hxt.1 (health) + wh-nvk (rows).
- The row-writer design (renderer + merge_markdown_patch caller + draft-PR path) with an impl follow-up
  ticket; arm-agnostic, sec-kb-refresh primary.
- The verdict path decided with wh-562 (no false-merit merges).
- A documented decision on RQ4 (retire rows) + RQ5 (sec-learn deferred + the capture-hook registration
  flagged as a pending human decision).
- ADR content: either a section in wh-562's watchlist-mechanism ADR or its own next-free ADR (decide at
  write time; never pre-claim a number). The ADR-015 'self-updates' design-intent clarification lands in
  whichever of wh-hxt.3's / this ADR ships it (exactly one).

## Time box
~1-2 days, AFTER wh-562's Gate-2 design (blocked-by).

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (G5, §2 limit CLOSED by the first-hand audit, §9.3b);
poll_feeds.py:23,69,82-107; patch_merge.py:40-58; harvest.py:18-38; capture_hooks.py; hooks.json;
phase-8-self-improvement.md:107; confine_self_writes.py:38-40; gate_kb_edit.py:18,40-49;
tool-registry.md:1-7 + change-log format; test_registry_lock.py; validate_kb.py (the validator pattern).
Siblings: wh-562 (Gate-2 — blocks this), wh-xn0 (admissibility screen spec), wh-hxt.1 (health/staleness
signal), wh-nvk (the rows that will first exercise the writer), wh-hxt.3 (strategy ADR).

title: Spike: replace Trivy with a diversified multi-vendor SCA+IaC set (Grype/Syft + Checkov + OSV-Scanner + gitleaks; maintained, MIT/Apache, CI-SHA-pinned)
description:
## Research Question

The user has **DECIDED to drop Trivy** (the `trivy-mcp` wrapper is unmaintained + pins a stale binary;
Trivy itself was TeamPCP-compromised — CVE-2026-33634 / GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23, see
`docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md`). This spike **picks the permanent replacement(s)**
behind the SCA + IaC + container-image + secrets + SBOM capabilities Trivy served, and **validates +
extends** the user's proposed diversified, multi-vendor set. It does NOT re-debate the drop-Trivy
decision — that is settled. Each RQ → a decision.

- **RQ1 — MAP the capabilities Trivy did for us.** `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` `sca`=`("trivy","*")`
  (`detect_tools.py:114`) + `iac` lead `("trivy","*")` (`:116`); registry SCA + IaC/container/CI lines
  (`tool-registry.md:30,38`) + the Trivy pin caveat (`:50-53`). Output = a capabilities-to-replace table
  (SCA multi-ecosystem · IaC/misconfig Dockerfile/k8s/Helm/Terraform/CloudFormation · container-image
  CVE · secrets · SBOM). → an authoritative "what must be covered" list.
- **RQ2 — VALIDATE the user's 5-tool set on 5 dimensions → a scorecard.** For Grype(+Syft), Checkov,
  kube-linter, gitleaks, OSV-Scanner, verify from UPSTREAM (LICENSE + release history, not a blog):
  (a) **License** MIT/Apache-2.0 ONLY (reject BSD/LGPL/GPL/AGPL); (b) **Maintenance/reliability** —
  cadence, last commit, maintainer health, **bus-factor** (the trivy-mcp staleness lesson is the headline
  criterion — be rigorously honest; FLAG gitleaks' single-maintainer footprint); (c) **Data-egress** —
  offline? telemetry default + disable (Checkov's Prisma/`--bc-api-key` — verify the offline default);
  (d) **CI** — official Action + plain binary, pinnable to a **commit-SHA not a mutable tag** (the Trivy
  force-push lesson), checksum/cosign/SLSA verify (ADR-006); (e) **Coverage parity** vs RQ1. → GO/EXTEND/REJECT per tool.
- **RQ3 — EXTEND: name any GAP + confirm the multi-vendor-diversity thesis.** Validate the thesis
  explicitly (a diversified multi-vendor set is a SECURITY UPGRADE — no single maintainer compromise
  takes out the pipeline; thin package-managed binaries, no vendored-old-MCP trap). State the
  **bus-factor caveat** (diversity raises the count of supply-chain surfaces → every tool must pass the
  same pin+verify gate). Gaps: GH-Actions hardening (actionlint/zizmor — MIT, already in the registry
  IaC line — keep); Dockerfile (hadolint = GPL reject → Checkov covers it); Terraform/CloudFormation
  (Checkov); a second-source for IaC (kube-linter k8s — optional). **EXCLUDE Checkmarx KICS** (same
  TeamPCP campaign). → final set + additions.
- **RQ4 — MCP vs CLI-first.** The trivy-mcp trap (unmaintained 3rd-party MCP pinning a stale binary)
  argues AGAINST any 3rd-party MCP dependency. CLI-first behind the capability interface (ADR-002 CLI-first/
  MCP-optional, ADR-015 capability layer, ADR-006 pin+verify). → each replacement = a pinned,
  checksum/sig-verified binary behind the capability port; NO MCP wrapper.
- **RQ5 — CI: per-tool Action pinned to commit-SHA + verify.** For each official Action: the **commit-SHA
  pin** (immutable; the `trivy-action` tags were force-pushed — only a SHA holds) + checksum/cosign/SLSA
  verify at install (ADR-006). For the binary path: pinned-version + checksum-verify-at-install (the
  "poisoned local binary" residual risk). → a per-tool CI pinning spec.
- **RQ6 — DECISION → impl.** Recommend the final set; specify: drop Trivy from `SCANNER_PREFERENCE`
  (`detect_tools.py:113-116`) + the `tool-registry.md` SCA/IaC lines; add the set with license/maintenance/
  data-egress columns; **handle the lock test** — `test_registry_lock.py:51` asserts the Trivy regex
  `r"0\.7[01]"`; it must be **removed/replaced** when the Trivy pin line is retired (else the lock goes
  red). Reconcile with the sibling spikes (Dependencies). → impl ticket(s).

## Timebox

**Timebox:** ~1–2 days (research artifacts, not production code).

## Background / Context

- **Decision made:** drop Trivy. Driver = (1) `trivy-mcp` unmaintained + pins a stale binary (the "MCP
  trap"); (2) Trivy upstream TeamPCP-compromised (CVE-2026-33634; force-pushed `trivy-action` tags +
  malicious binary v0.69.4 / images v0.69.5–.6). Verification + our (LOW–MEDIUM, partial) exposure are
  FINAL in `docs/research/20260609_trivy_teampcp_supply_chain.md`.
- **User's proposed set (the hypothesis):** Grype(+Syft) [Anchore] container/OCI image CVE+SBOM ·
  Checkov [Palo Alto] IaC/k8s/Helm/Terraform/Dockerfile misconfig · kube-linter [Red Hat] k8s-only
  optional · gitleaks [independent] secrets · OSV-Scanner [Google] dependency-vuln cross-check.
- **User's thesis (validate — it is strong):** a diversified multi-vendor set is a SECURITY UPGRADE over
  one do-everything tool; no vendored-old-version MCP layer; each CI-native + specialized.
- **VALIDATION DIGEST (verified upstream 2026-06-09 — confirm + cite in the report):** Grype Apache-2.0
  v0.110.0 cosign-signed (GO) · Syft Apache-2.0 v1.45.1 (GO) · Checkov Apache-2.0 3.2.533 ~2-4d cadence,
  Gate-2 PASS offline w/o `--bc-api-key` (GO) · kube-linter Apache-2.0 v0.8.3 ~3mo (EXTEND, optional) ·
  gitleaks MIT v8.30.1 single-maintainer/feature-complete→Betterleaks (GO + bus-factor caveat) ·
  OSV-Scanner Apache-2.0 v2.3.8 (GO) · KICS REJECT (TeamPCP). trufflehog AGPL = Gate-1 fail (not a swap).
- **Constraining ADRs:** ADR-002 (CLI-first/MCP-optional, `ARD.md:28`) · ADR-003 (degrade, `:39`) ·
  ADR-006 (pin+verify, `:66`) · ADR-015 (swappable capability, `:149`) · ADR-019 (`:244`). **Last WRITTEN
  ADR = ADR-023** (`:281`).
- **Honesty constraints (user-stated, load-bearing):** maintenance/reliability + telemetry are the two
  decision drivers — report rigorously, incl. gitleaks' bus-factor + Checkov's Prisma-API posture.

## Investigation Approach

1. RQ1 — read `detect_tools.py:110-119`, `tool-registry.md:30,38,50-53`; build the capabilities-to-replace table.
2. RQ2 — verify each tool upstream (LICENSE/SPDX + GitHub API `published_at` + release notes); fill the scorecard.
3. RQ3 — gaps (Actions hardening / Dockerfile / Terraform / second-source IaC); confirm the diversity thesis + bus-factor caveat; reject KICS.
4. RQ4/RQ5 — CLI-first (no MCP) + per-tool commit-SHA pin + checksum/cosign verify.
5. RQ6 — impl spec (registry + `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` + the `r"0\.7[01]"` lock fix); create follow-up ticket(s); reconcile with the sibling spikes.

## Dependencies / reconcile

- **wh-d5b** (interim quarantine — demote Trivy now): land it first (hours, gated by the lock test); this
  spike makes the removal PERMANENT + names the replacement (supersedes wh-d5b's "returns when cleared").
- **wh-hxt.3** (CONTAIN — RE-POINTED 2026-06-09 per the loop-leverage audit): the per-replacement
  ARTIFACT pin+verify (RQ5 — commit-SHA/digest/checksum + cosign) plugs into wh-hxt.3's RQ4 artifact-
  provenance admission arm under CONTAIN — do not invent a second mechanism. wh-562 is referenced ONLY
  for watchlist ENTRIES (its re-scoped Gate-2 is a DATA gate for watchlist/registry rows — a different
  object kind; the Trivy watchlist entry is shared with wh-k6l).
- **wh-xn0** (admissibility: MIT/Apache-only license + local/no-telemetry data-egress gates): every
  replacement MUST pass wh-xn0's two gates; RQ2 (a)+(c) ARE those gates — cite, don't re-derive.

## Expected Deliverables

- [ ] Research report `docs/research/20260609_trivy_replacement_sca_iac.md` — the scorecard +
      coverage-parity matrix + the diversity-thesis verdict; every claim sourced to an upstream URL.
- [ ] An **ADR at the NEXT AVAILABLE number at write time** appended to `docs/ARD.md` (last written =
      ADR-023; 024/025 are CLAIMED by in-flight spikes wh-xn0 + the GDPR spike but NOT yet written — do
      NOT hard-code; take the next free number when you write). Decision = "drop Trivy; adopt the
      diversified split."
- [ ] Impl follow-up ticket(s): remove Trivy from `SCANNER_PREFERENCE` (`detect_tools.py:113-116`) + the
      `tool-registry.md` SCA/IaC lines; add the set with license/maintenance/data-egress columns;
      **remove/replace the `r"0\.7[01]"` assertion at `test_registry_lock.py:51`**; per-tool commit-SHA
      pin + checksum/cosign verify wired through wh-hxt.3's RQ4 artifact-provenance arm. **The registry
      rewrite is SHARED with wh-xn0's impl** — one combined ticket (its columns + these rows + the lock
      fix) or explicit file-sequencing; never two uncoordinated writers to the same two files.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RQ1–RQ6 each answered with a decision.
- [ ] Scorecard fully populated; every license/maintenance/telemetry claim cited to an UPSTREAM source.
- [ ] Coverage parity vs Trivy shown (no silent gap); KICS explicitly excluded (TeamPCP).
- [ ] Multi-vendor-diversity thesis validated WITH the bus-factor caveat (gitleaks single-maintainer).
- [ ] CLI-first / no-MCP confirmed (ADR-002/006/015); per-tool commit-SHA pin + verify specified (ADR-006).
- [ ] Report + ADR (next-available number) written; impl ticket(s) created; reconciled with wh-d5b/wh-562/wh-xn0.
- [ ] The `test_registry_lock.py:51` Trivy-regex handling is named in the impl spec.
- [ ] Timebox respected or extended with justification.

## Expected Outcome (valid result)

"Use the diversified split — **Grype (+Syft) + Checkov + OSV-Scanner + gitleaks**, with **kube-linter**
(k8s) + **actionlint/zizmor** (GH-Actions) as needed" is the expected, valid outcome. The spike grounds
it with verified upstream evidence + wires pin+verify per tool — it does not relitigate dropping Trivy.

## Notes / Open Questions

- gitleaks feature-complete (security patches only; author → "Betterleaks") — admit now (MIT, patched,
  mature domain), track Betterleaks via `sec-kb-refresh`. trufflehog (the obvious alt) is AGPL = Gate-1 fail.
- Checkov: run **offline, without `--bc-api-key`** to keep Gate-2 (no source upload / Prisma enrich).
  Confirm the exact offline invocation in the report.
- ADR race: three spikes target 024/025 — coordinate the final number AT WRITE TIME, don't pre-assign.

title: Two white-hacker agents collide on name: (shipped vs dogfood) - de-collide + fix stale CLAUDE.md Architecture claim
description:
Two files both declare `name: white-hacker` in frontmatter:
- plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md (15984B) = the SHIPPED generic security reviewer for ANY repo. Has when_to_use, kind/phase/license/memory frontmatter, tools incl. ToolSearch, the full OWASP review loop, and the "## Execution budget" section (line 145).
- .claude/agents/white-hacker.md (14567B) = a PROJECT-LOCAL dogfood dev-team reviewer of white-hackers OWN injection surfaces (confine hooks, F-001 allowlist, gate_kb_edit, eval-corpus isolation). Tools OMIT ToolSearch; has a "## Resource discipline" section instead of Execution budget. Sits beside developer/devops/qa-engineer/tech-lead/researcher/project-manager.md (the whole team).

These are NOT drifted copies of one agent. Evidence they are INTENTIONALLY DIFFERENT:
- git history: common ancestor f0adb40 (initial) + 4a2c46b (Phase 10 packaging) touched both; then b3325cf "add dev-team agent profiles" turned the .claude/ copy into a team profile, while e48887b/4f51920 ("in the SHIPPED agent too") evolved the plugins/ copy separately.
- layout: .claude/agents/ is the team-profile dir; plugins/white-hacker/agents/ ships exactly one agent.
- wh-00i groom note already records this split and scopes itself to ONLY the SHIPPED agent.

=> DO NOT symlink or regenerate one from the other. That would destroy the dogfood agent.

Real defects to fix:
1. NAME COLLISION (reachable): both are `name: white-hacker`. devops.md:154,174 dogfood via `claude --plugin-dir ./plugins/white-hacker`, which loads BOTH agents into one session under the same name -> one shadows the other / ambiguous subagent_type.
2. STALE CANONICAL DOC: .claude/CLAUDE.md "Architecture at a glance" says: "One agent .claude/agents/white-hacker.md (the senior-security-engineer identity + stage dispatch)". Wrong on both counts - the senior-security-engineer generic identity lives at plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md; the .claude/ file is the dogfood teammate; and there is not "one" agent.
design:
DECISION (GROOMED): NOT symlink, NOT regenerate. The two agents are intentionally distinct AND consistent with ADR-017 (.claude/ = dev scaffolding vs plugins/<name>/ = shipped payload, docs/ARD.md:199): the dev tree carries a TEAM of dev agents incl. a dogfood white-hacker; the payload ships ONE generic agent. Defects are narrow: (1) both got `name: white-hacker`; (2) ADR-009 + .claude/CLAUDE.md:57 were never updated after ADR-017 relocated the shipped identity to plugins/.

Reconciliation (3 parts):
1. APPEND a new ADR (next ADR-0NN; ADRs are append-only, Policy 11 - do NOT edit ADR-009/017). Record the two-agent reality: shipped generic = plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md (ADR-017 payload); dogfood dev teammate = .claude/agents/<new-name>.md (ADR-017 .claude/=dev). State it clarifies/supersedes ADR-009 ("identity once at .claude/agents/white-hacker.md" - path relocated by ADR-017, single-agent framing now two). Decide the de-collision here; cite ADR-004 (identity preservation, docs/ARD.md:47).
2. DE-COLLIDE the dogfood agent: rename .claude/agents/white-hacker.md `name:` (e.g. white-hacker-selfreview) [optionally the filename] so it does not shadow the shipped agent during the documented dogfood `claude --plugin-dir ./plugins/white-hacker` (ADR-017, docs/ARD.md:210). Update launch-team.md team table (line 78) + teammate refs (48,87,88,103,156,186) + devops.md + researcher.md.
3. SYNC derived docs: .claude/CLAUDE.md:57 "Architecture at a glance" -> describe BOTH agents at their correct paths (file is 91 lines now; keep <200, Policy 6).

OPEN (settle at claim, does NOT block grooming): confirm how Claude Code resolves a plugin agent vs a project agent sharing `name:` under --plugin-dir (1-command smoke in this repo, or ask claude-code-guide). If CC already namespaces them benignly, step 2 may reduce to a doc note; the ADR (1) + doc sync (3) still stand.

OUT OF SCOPE: wh-00i (edits ONLY the shipped agent Execution-budget section).
acceptance_criteria:
- No two simultaneously-loadable agents share `name: white-hacker` (verified by loading both via --plugin-dir), OR the collision is documented benign with the actual Claude Code resolution behavior cited.
- .claude/CLAUDE.md "Architecture at a glance" names BOTH agents at their correct paths (shipped generic vs dogfood teammate); no longer claims .claude/agents/white-hacker.md is the senior-security-engineer identity.
- launch-team.md / devops.md / researcher.md references to the dogfood agent are consistent with whatever name is chosen.
- .claude/CLAUDE.md stays < 200 lines.
- pytest + `claude plugin validate ./plugins/white-hacker` stay green.
notes:
GROOMING REPORT wh-g2v (2026-06-09) - VERDICT: READY.
- Files real: plugins/white-hacker/agents/white-hacker.md (15984B), .claude/agents/white-hacker.md (14567B), .claude/commands/launch-team.md, .claude/CLAUDE.md (91 lines, <200 OK). All exist.
- KEY GROOM FINDING: the "one agent" claim is anchored in ADR-009 (docs/ARD.md:105-110, accepted) - "Define the identity once (.claude/agents/white-hacker.md)". ADR-017 (docs/ARD.md:199-230, ".claude/ dev vs plugins/ payload") relocated the shipped identity to plugins/ AND documents the `--plugin-dir` dogfood (line 210) that makes the name collision reachable. ADR-004 (line 47) = identity preservation. => reconciliation needs an APPENDED ADR + de-collision + doc sync, NOT a one-line edit. Design updated accordingly.
- Active agent registry in this dev session resolves `white-hacker` to the DOGFOOD copy (tools Read/Grep/Glob/Bash/SendMessage, no ToolSearch) - confirms .claude/ project agent shadows in dev; the plugin copy is source-under-development, not installed.
- Scope: agents/ + docs/ARD.md + .claude/CLAUDE.md + launch-team.md (no skill package, no _shared, no schema, no finding-schema/kb_refs). Single coherent concern. No bd blockers. NOT wh-00i. Related: wh-3sl (stale skills/ dev-paths).

title: KB: AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 — TeamPCP imposter-commit / tag-force-push / CI-secret-pivot technique
description:
## Goal
Capture the TeamPCP technique as a dated, sourced KB entry **AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002** (under the ADR-019
supply-chain technique_class): compromising widely-used dev/security tooling supply chains via
imposter-commit / tag force-push / CI-secret pivot (Trivy, LiteLLM, Telnyx, @tanstack, Nx Console), so
the inner loop watches for it. (wh-562 RQ-E delegates the KB deliverable here.)

## Prerequisites
- ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain.md holds AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-001 (slopsquatting); ADR-019 added the
  supply-chain technique_class.
- KB entries are human-gated draft-PR (ADR-012) + schema-valid (validate_kb): mandatory metadata.source,
  url, retrieved.
- **GROOM-PROVED DEFECT (folded in 2026-06-09): do NOT append -002 to reference/supply-chain.md.**
  validate_kb.split_front_matter stops at the FIRST closing fence — an appended second entry becomes
  BODY, never schema/id/size-checked, while validate_kb exits 0 (FALSE-GREEN). Convention
  (SKILL.md:27-28/:46) = ONE entry per file.

## Steps
1. **CREATE A NEW FILE `ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain-2.md`** for AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002: the
   technique (imposter-commit / tag-force-push / CI-secret pivot to weaponize widely-used tooling) +
   technique-level detections (pin to commit-SHA/digest NOT tags; checksum/signature verify; a
   known-compromised watchlist; "force-push defeats version-tag pinning").
   Cross-refs — **each advisory id PAIRED with its product**: Trivy: CVE-2026-33634 /
   GHSA-69fq-xp46-6x23; LiteLLM: GHSA-5mg7-485q-xm76; Nx Console: GHSA-c9j4-9m59-847w / CVE-2026-48027;
   plus OWASP LLM03. Dated + sourced.
2. Validate **with pyyaml**: `uv run --project plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/scripts --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/scripts/validate_kb.py` — and **assert the NEW id AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 appears in the validator output** (a pass without the id listed = the false-green; fail loud).
3. Route via the sec-kb-refresh draft-PR gate (never auto-merge, ADR-012).

## Files to Modify
- NEW plugins/white-hacker/skills/ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain-2.md (one entry per file — never append to supply-chain.md)

## Verification
- validate_kb passes AND its output lists AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 (no false-green); unique dated id;
  mandatory source/url/retrieved present; size caps hold

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AISEC-SUPPLY-CHAIN-002 in a NEW reference/supply-chain-2.md (not an append)
- [ ] technique + technique-level detections + dated sources; every advisory id paired with its product
- [ ] validate_kb (with pyyaml) green AND the new id asserted in its output
- [ ] human-gated draft-PR, never auto-merged (ADR-012)

## Rollback
`git revert` the KB entry file.

## Notes
Implements wh-562 RQ-E (delegated). The detector CODE (watchlist/Gate-2) is separate (wh-k6l + wh-562) —
the KB documents the TECHNIQUE (KB-as-knowledge / detector-as-code split, Rule 5).

## References
docs/research/20260609_*.md; ADR-019, ADR-012; ai-attack-kb/reference/supply-chain.md (the -001
precedent + the one-entry-per-file convention); validate_kb.py split_front_matter (the false-green
mechanics); wh-562 (RQ-E).
notes: GROOM 2026-06-09 (wave-1a pass — all claims re-verified live): KB package baseline GREEN (63 passed); whole-KB validator run GREEN ('OK reference', RC=0). Confirmed: supply-chain-2.md does NOT exist (greenfield); the split naming IS the documented convention (SKILL.md:27-28 'a class that grows splits to reference/<class>-<n>.md'); split_front_matter:59-70 stops at the FIRST closing fence (false-green on append — proved again); -001 (reference/supply-chain.md) is the exact front-matter contract to copy (id/title/technique_class/severity/confidence/status/date/modified/review_by/metadata.source+url+retrieved/supersedes/detections[]/xref[]); size caps = <=120-word first-paragraph summary + <=400-line file (validate_kb.py:7,29); dir-run enforces cross-file UNIQUE id (validate_kb.py:171-187). PROBE CORRECTION (the ticket's 'assert the new id appears in validator output' is UNSATISFIABLE — success output is only 'OK <path>', no id listing): replace with the two-run probe — (1) FILE run: uv run --project .../scripts --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python validate_kb.py reference/supply-chain-2.md -> 'OK ...supply-chain-2.md' RC=0 (proves THE NEW FILE's front-matter parsed + schema/size-valid — defeats the false-green directly); (2) DIR run on reference/ -> OK RC=0 (proves cross-file id uniqueness + no regression). ADR-012/019 resolve. READY with the probe correction.

title: Spike: continuous supply-chain-compromise monitoring (target deps + IDE/VS Code extensions vs a shared watchlist; install + periodic)
description:
> **Scope:** This spike protects the **USER's project + machine**. It is the *sibling* of wh-562 (Gate-2,
> the DATA gate for the shared watchlist). The two SHARE one watchlist/feed mechanism — **do not build a
> second feed**. Reconcile, don't fork (ADR-015).
>
> **REFINED 2026-06-09** per the loop-leverage audit: the OSSF FEEDER is promoted to the PRIMARY
> deliverable (it was only in the notes); RQ3 (version-aware S8) is DELEGATED to wh-4k9 (exists, groomed
> READY — consume, don't re-decide); the schema ownership is settled (RQ2 here FINALIZES the OSV-superset;
> wh-k6l instantiates the file; wh-562's Gate-2 validates it); the separate monitoring ADR FOLDS INTO
> wh-562's one watchlist-mechanism ADR (one watchlist -> one ADR — this spike's design feeds it).
>
> **Verify-first status (CONFIRMED upstream, 2026-06-09 — keep, re-cite, don't re-debate):**
> LiteLLM PyPI v1.82.7/1.82.8 (GHSA-5mg7-485q-xm76); Telnyx PyPI v4.87.1/4.87.2; ~42 `@tanstack/*` npm
> pkgs / 84 versions (Mini Shai-Hulud, 2026-05-11); ~3,800 GitHub **internal** repos exfiltrated via the
> poisoned **Nx Console** VS Code extension nrwl.angular-console v18.95.0 (GHSA-c9j4-9m59-847w /
> CVE-2026-48027) — on top of the confirmed Trivy CVE-2026-33634. RQ1 verdicts are RECORDED FINAL in
> docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md.

## PRIMARY deliverable — the OSSF feeder (loop-leverage G1, P0)

/sec-kb-refresh does NOT feed the watchlist today — poll_feeds.py:69 PARSERS={osv,atlas,atom} (no OSSF
parser); :23 VALID_CLASSES = 5 KB stems (no supply-chain); it renders KB markdown only (:82-107). Yet
MALWARE-DB.md:48,79 + deps-scan SKILL.md:110 NAME /sec-kb-refresh as the re-pin cadence — prose with zero
code. CLOSE THE GAP: add an OSSF-malicious-packages parser to poll_feeds PARSERS + a watchlist-candidate
writer that emits ONLY `{source-url (GHSA/OSV), package, version, ecosystem, retrieved}` — never
free-form advisory prose — as a human-gated draft PR in the shared OSV-superset shape. Rule-of-Two
preserved: reuse the safe_dump untrusted-feed handling (test_poll_feeds.py:66-95 pins
poisoned-feed-cannot-inject); the OSSF host is reachable (github.com already in FEED_HOSTS; git is not a
NET_VERB); keep the fetch(network-on)/analyze(network-off) split (fetch-snapshot.sh). Every candidate
must pass wh-562's Gate-2 validator — schema-first ordering: Gate-2's schema lands before this feeder
writes entries.

## Research questions (remaining)
- **RQ2 — the shared WATCHLIST schema (THIS spike FINALIZES it).** OSV-superset so load_malware_db still
  parses the package kind unchanged; add `ecosystem`; THREE entry kinds behind one shape with a
  `target:{dependency|tool|extension}` tag (packages / extensions / repos — repos likely DROP for a
  per-user tool). wh-k6l's file is the concrete instance; wh-562's Gate-2 validates; ONE
  file/feed/updater. Human-gated draft-PR only (ADR-012); pin+verify each snapshot per ADR-006 (the
  fetch-snapshot.sh pattern — already SHA-verified, do not rebuild).
- **RQ3 — DELEGATED to wh-4k9** (version-aware S8: is_known_bad + lockfile-resolved versions; the
  name-only match is at supply_chain.py:754). Keep here ONLY the ecosystem-key disambiguation + optional
  digest-match aspects, which belong to the RQ2 schema.
- **RQ4 — NEW SURFACE: the IDE/VS Code EXTENSION scan.** Enumerate installed extensions
  (`code --list-extensions --show-versions`, fall back to on-disk dirs; no code + no dir ->
  tools_unavailable, ADR-003) + check vs the `extensions` watchlist kind (reuse is_known_bad). LEAN: a
  thin module behind a NEW `ide-hygiene` capability port (ADR-015); port =
  `list_installed_extensions() -> [{marketplace,id,version}]`. Machine scan -> RQ7.
- **RQ5 — CADENCE (the key open USER decision).** No daemon/cron//loop (standing aversion). RECOMMENDED:
  on-demand command + a FAST SessionStart watchlist-drift check — bounded (pinned watchlist + the
  already-parsed lockfile + one `code --list-extensions`; no recursive HOME walk), FACTUAL
  additionalContext (ADR-017, <=10k), silent degrade. CI opt-in. (User may prefer CI-only — decide
  before execution.)
- **RQ6 — INSTALL-TIME WIRING into sec-init (wh-0vx).** A FACTUAL summary line in the companion ("0
  installed deps/extensions match the 2026-06 watchlist"); watchlist DATA stays pinned in reference/,
  never in the committed profile. Don't collide with wh-0vx's in-flight schema keys.
- **RQ7 — PRIVACY boundary (machine scan; reconcile wh-81y).** One-time explicit consent; read-only +
  no-egress (offline pinned watchlist); data minimization — surface only MATCHES, never store/transmit
  the extension inventory; PUBLIC threat-intel in, user data never out.

## Expected deliverables
- [ ] The OSSF feeder design (parser + candidate writer + draft-PR path), Gate-2-validated, with an impl
      follow-up ticket. (PRIMARY)
- [ ] The finalized OSV-superset schema (RQ2) — handed to wh-k6l (file) + wh-562 (validator + the ONE ADR).
- [ ] RQ4 extension-scan decision (capability port + enumerator + degrade path); PoC only if load-bearing.
- [ ] RQ5 cadence recommendation honoring the scheduler-aversion + resource caps.
- [ ] RQ6 sec-init wiring + RQ7 privacy boundary, reconciled with wh-0vx + wh-81y.
- [ ] NO separate ADR — this spike's design feeds wh-562's one watchlist-mechanism ADR.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Feeder: poll_feeds gains the OSSF parser + the minimal-field candidate writer (design + impl
      ticket); emits only {source-url,package,version,ecosystem,retrieved}; safe_dump handling reused;
      fetch/analyze split kept; draft-PR human-gated
- [ ] Schema: OSV-superset finalized, ONE watchlist (target tag), load_malware_db-compatible; explicitly
      consumed by wh-k6l + validated by wh-562's Gate-2
- [ ] RQ3 delegated to wh-4k9 (no double-implementation); only ecosystem/digest schema aspects kept here
- [ ] RQ4/RQ5/RQ6/RQ7 each -> a decision (extension port; cadence; install wiring; privacy boundary)
- [ ] Timebox (~1-2 days) respected

## References
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_compromise_monitoring.md (RQ1 FINAL + RQ2-RQ7 design home);
docs/research/20260609_supply_chain_loop_leverage.md (G1 + §4.3 Rule-of-Two constraints + §9.1);
poll_feeds.py:23,69,82-107; test_poll_feeds.py:66-95; malware_db.py:26,47; supply_chain.py:754;
fetch-snapshot.sh; MALWARE-DB.md:48,79. Siblings: wh-562 (Gate-2 + the ONE ADR), wh-k6l (the file),
wh-4k9 (S8 — delegated), wh-0vx (sec-init), wh-81y (privacy), wh-hxt.1 (staleness signal arm — wh-5es
owns the poller plumbing, wh-hxt.1 the staleness signal definition). ADR-003/006/012/015/017/019.
notes:
LOOP-WIRING AUDIT 2026-06-09 (workflow wst324yuh, adversarially verified): /sec-kb-refresh does NOT feed the watchlist today — poll_feeds.py:69 PARSERS={osv,atlas,atom} (no OSSF parser), :23 VALID_CLASSES = 5 KB stems (no "supply-chain"); renders KB markdown only (:82-107). Docs NAME /sec-kb-refresh as the OSSF re-pin cadence (MALWARE-DB.md:48,79; deps-scan SKILL.md:110) but there is ZERO code. SCOPE: add an OSSF-malicious-packages parser to poll_feeds PARSERS + a candidate writer emitting ONLY {source-url(GHSA/OSV), package, version, ecosystem, retrieved} as a draft PR (reuse safe_dump untrusted-feed handling; confidence-low/AUTO-EXTRACTED). Rule-of-Two OK: OSSF host reachable (github.com already in FEED_HOSTS; git not a NET_VERB); keep fetch(net-on)/analyze(net-none) split. Pairs with wh-562 Gate-2 — ONE watchlist/format.

WAVE-1B QA NITS PARKED HERE 2026-06-10 (the RQ2 ecosystem-key residual class — from the wh-7o1 review): (1) Gemfile.lock PLATFORM-SUFFIXED versions attach verbatim ('nokogiri (1.15.4-arm64-darwin)' -> resolved='1.15.4-arm64-darwin') while OSV-Ruby records canonical '1.15.4' -> narrow FN when ONLY a platform row exists (generic row wins when both present — probed). Fix candidate: strip the trailing -<platform> suffix in _resolved_gem, decided alongside the ecosystem key. (2) Maven ${prop} resolution is intentionally ONE-LEVEL (chained ${a}->${b} stays wildcard-only; probed loop-safe) — needs a one-line docstring note when this area is next touched. Both fail-safe (FN-only, human-triaged S8), neither blocks wave 1b.

GROOM 2026-06-10 (pre-claim, W-next pass at HEAD 338d667 + the uncommitted wave-1b tree): VERDICT READY. All feeder-gap cites EXACT and still true: poll_feeds.py:23 VALID_CLASSES (5 stems, no supply-chain), :69 PARSERS={osv,atlas,atom}, :82-107 KB-render-only; test_poll_feeds.py:66 poisoned-feed test; FEED_HOSTS confine_self_writes.py:41; si-07-threat-feeds.md exists; package baseline GREEN (9 passed). CORRECTIONS: (1) RQ3 is now fully DELIVERED, not merely delegated — wh-4k9 AND wh-7o1 are CLOSED; signal_s8 (now supply_chain.py:1015) is VERSION-AWARE across npm/pypi/go/maven/ruby/cargo+pipfile; the body's two 'name-only @ :754' references are stale — read them as historical context; only the ecosystem-key + digest schema aspects remain here (RQ2). (2) Cite drift: load_malware_db is :27, is_known_bad :48. (3) NEW BINDING: ADR-024 (uncommitted, wave-1b) ratified CONTAIN — the feeder MUST keep the fetch(network-on)/analyze(network-off) split (now an ADR requirement, not just a pattern) and cite ADR-024 in the design; the no-separate-ADR plan (folds into wh-562's watchlist ADR) is consistent with ADR-024's three-gates split. (4) The parked nits in this ticket's notes (Gemfile platform-suffix FN; Maven one-level prop resolution) are RQ2 schema inputs — address the platform-suffix question in the OSV-superset ecosystem-key design.
